own_review.point: Typo 'stanard input/output' should be 'standard input/output' in the new description
'server side events' is an incorrect expansion of SSE (it stands for Server-Sent Events); appears in both the sse pom description and the yaml name, user-facing on Maven Central/code.quarkus

New quarkus-extension.yaml files omit the top-level 'description:' field that core/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml has; relies on pom description inheritance instead of matching the core extension's convention

Descriptions added to parent aggregator poms (packaging pom), not runtime artifact poms; works because Maven inherits <description>, but all child artifacts (deployment too) get the same description — minor placement/convention note

Guide URL (docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-mcp-server/dev/index.html) differs from the commented-out URL in core's yaml (docs.quarkiverse.io/mcp-server/dev/); worth verifying which is correct and aligning core

points.why: Verified in diff; matches the human review's suggestion.

points.point: sse yaml missing top-level description field present in core yaml
points.why: Verified: core yaml has description:, new sse yaml does not; concrete consistency fix aligned with the PR's purpose.

points.point: stdio yaml missing description field, same convention
points.why: Same verified gap in the second new yaml file.

missed: 'server side events' is a wrong expansion of SSE (Server-Sent Events) in pom description and yaml name

points.point: Typo 'stanard' -> 'standard', noting sibling SSE pom spells it correctly
points.why: Verified; matches human suggestion, with accurate sibling comparison.

points.point: sse yaml missing top-level description field vs core yaml
points.why: Verified against core/runtime yaml; concrete actionable fix.

points.point: stdio yaml missing top-level description field
points.why: Same verified gap in the second file.

points.point: Guide URL differs from core's commented-out URL path; verify it matches intended docs structure
points.why: Verified discrepancy (mcp-server vs quarkus-mcp-server path); the guide line is exactly what the human review focused on, and v2 characterizes it accurately as low priority.

missed: 'server side events' is a wrong expansion of SSE (Server-Sent Events) in pom description and yaml name

points.point: sse yaml missing description field vs core extension yaml
points.why: Verified consistency gap with core yaml.
Same verified gap in the second file.

missed: 'server side events' is a wrong expansion of SSE (Server-Sent Events) in pom description and yaml name

points.point: sse yaml missing description:, claiming the catalog 'will have no description to display'
points.why: The missing field vs core is verified and actionable, though the impact claim overstates it (the extension descriptor falls back to the inherited pom description).
Same verified gap in the second file.

points.point: Guide URL differs from core's commented-out path; confirm docs exist, else comment it out like core
points.why: Real verified discrepancy and properly hedged, though the 'comment it out' suggestion runs opposite to the human reviewer's note that the guide is already published.

missed: 'server side events' is a wrong expansion of SSE (Server-Sent Events) in pom description and yaml name

qualitative: All four candidates caught the two substantive issues: the 'stanard' typo (the human's concrete fix) and the missing top-level description in the new quarkus-extension.yaml files compared with core's yaml — a real convention gap I also found independently. v2 and v4 additionally flagged the guide-URL inconsistency with core's commented-out link, which is the exact line the human review touched, making them the most complete; v2 edges out v4 qualitatively because v4 slightly overclaims the impact of the missing yaml description and suggests commenting out a guide that the human reviewer notes is already published. The human's guide-URL suggestion text is already present verbatim in this diff snapshot, so I did not count it as missed for v1/v3. No candidate noticed that 'server side events' is a wrong expansion of SSE (Server-Sent Events) in the user-facing descriptions, the one finding unique to my review. No fabrications anywhere; this was a small metadata PR and all reviews stayed grounded.
